title: Songs Of The Spring Days
content:
В В В В В В В В I.

В В В В В В В В A gentle wind, of western birth
В В В В В В В В On some far summer sea,
В В В В В В В В Wakes daisies in the wintry earth,
В В В В В В В В Wakes hopes in wintry me.

В В В В В В В В The sun is low; the paths are wet,
В В В В В В В В And dance with frolic hail;
В В В В В В В В The trees--their spring-time is not yet--
В В В В В В В В Swing sighing in the gale.

В В В В В В В В Young gleams of sunshine peep and play;
В В В В В В В В Clouds shoulder in between;
В В В В В В В В I scarce believe one coming day
В В В В В В В В The earth will all be green.

В В В В В В В В The north wind blows, and blasts, and raves,
В В В В В В В В And flaps his snowy wing:
В В В В В В В В Back! toss thy bergs on arctic waves;
В В В В В В В В Thou canst not bar our spring.


В В В В В В В В II.

В В В В В В В В Up comes the primrose, wondering;
В В В В В В В В The snowdrop droopeth by;
В В В В В В В В The holy spirit of the spring
В В В В В В В В Is working silently.

В В В В В В В В Soft-breathing breezes woo and wile
В В В В В В В В The later children out;
В В В В В В В В O'er woods and farms a sunny smile
В В В В В В В В Is flickering about.

В В В В В В В В The earth was cold, hard-hearted, dull;
В В В В В В В В To death almost she slept:
В В В В В В В В Over her, heaven grew beautiful,
В В В В В В В В And forth her beauty crept.

В В В В В В В В Showers yet must fall, and waters grow
В В В В В В В В Dark-wan with furrowing blast;
В В В В В В В В But suns will shine, and soft winds blow,
В В В В В В В В Till the year flowers at last.


В В В В В В В В III.

В В В В В В В В The sky is smiling over me,
В В В В В В В В Hath smiled away the frost;
В В В В В В В В White daisies star the sky-like lea,
В В В В В В В В With buds the wood's embossed.

В В В В В В В В Troops of wild flowers gaze at the sky
В В В В В В В В Up through the latticed boughs;
В В В В В В В В Till comes the green cloud by and by,
В В В В В В В В It is not time to house.

В В В В В В В В Yours is the day, sweet bird--sing on;
В В В В В В В В The winter is forgot;
В В В В В В В В Like an ill dream 'tis over and gone:
В В В В В В В В Pain that is past, is not.

В В В В В В В В Joy that was past is yet the same:
В В В В В В В В If care the summer brings,
В В В В В В В В 'Twill only be another name
В В В В В В В В For love that broods, not sings.


В В В В В В В В IV.

В В В В В В В В Blow on me, wind, from west and south;
В В В В В В В В Sweet summer-spirit, blow!
В В В В В В В В Come like a kiss from dear child's mouth,
В В В В В В В В Who knows not what I know.

В В В В В В В В The earth's perfection dawneth soon;
В В В В В В В В Ours lingereth alway;
В В В В В В В В We have a morning, not a noon;
В В В В В В В В Spring, but no summer gay.

В В В В В В В В Rose-blotted eve, gold-branded morn
В В В В В В В В Crown soon the swift year's life:
В В В В В В В В In us a higher hope is born,
В В В В В В В В And claims a longer strife.

В В В В В В В В Will heaven be an eternal spring
В В В В В В В В With summer at the door?
В В В В В В В В Or shall we one day tell its king
В В В В В В В В That we desire no more?
meaning:
This evocative piece by George MacDonald represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of George MacDonald's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
